title: Authors and Citation
text: Jimmy Briggs. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Briggs J (2026). gdalviz: Visualize Modern GDAL Pipelines. R package version 0.0.0.9000, http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalviz/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {gdalviz: Visualize Modern GDAL Pipelines},   author = {Jimmy Briggs},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {http://docs.jimbrig.com/gdalviz/}, }

title: Visualize Modern GDAL Pipelines
text: gdalviz provides modern framework parsing, validating, visualizing GDAL pipeline algorithms (example gdal vector pipeline ! ... GDALG command_line definitions).

title: Visualize Modern GDAL Pipelines
text: can install development version gdalviz like :

title: Visualize Modern GDAL Pipelines
text: Parse, validate, render modern GDAL vector pipeline: use new react-flow based renderer:
code: library(gdalviz)  cmd <- paste(   "gdal vector pipeline",   "read --input /data/parcels.gpkg --input-layer parcels",   "! filter --where \"statefp = '13'\"",   "! make-valid",   "! reproject --output-crs EPSG:4326",   "! write --output /tmp/parcels.fgb --output-format FlatGeobuf" )  p <- parse_pipeline(cmd) v <- validate_pipeline(p, strict = FALSE) issues <- lint_pipeline(p)  g <- pipeline_graph(p) render_diagrammer(g) pkg_sys_extdata("pipelines/tiger_states.gdalg.json") |>     pipeline_graph() |>     render_reactflow(theme = "dark", minimap = FALSE, direction = "TB")

title: Convert a pipeline to a GDALG specification list — as_gdalg

text: x gdalviz_pipeline, string/path accepted read_gdalg(). relative_paths Logical relative_paths_relative_to_this_file member. Defaults TRUE. gdal_version Optional GDAL version string record.

text: named list type, command_line, optional members, suitable serialization *.gdalg.json file.

what: gdalviz: Visualize Modern GDAL Pipelines — gdalviz-package
title: gdalviz: Visualize Modern GDAL Pipelines — gdalviz-package
text: Produces interactive static visualizations modern GDAL CLI pipelines.

title: gdalviz: Visualize Modern GDAL Pipelines — gdalviz-package
text: Maintainer: Jimmy Briggs jimmy.briggs@jimbrig.com (ORCID) Authors: Jimmy Briggs jimmy.briggs@jimbrig.com (ORCID)

what: GDAL vector pipeline contract registry — gdalviz_contract
title: GDAL vector pipeline contract registry — gdalviz_contract
text: Loads bundled gdal vector pipeline --json-usage snapshot turns lookup pipeline steps arguments. single source truth steps arguments valid, types, choices, documentation URLs.

title: GDAL vector pipeline contract registry — gdalviz_contract
GDAL vector pipeline contract registry — gdalviz_contract
text: path Optional path --json-usage JSON snapshot. Defaults snapshot bundled package. refresh TRUE, bypass cache reload.

title: GDAL vector pipeline contract registry — gdalviz_contract
text: gdalviz_contract object: named list step definitions.

what: Regenerate the GDAL pipeline contract snapshot from the installed GDAL — gdalviz_refresh_contract
title: Regenerate the GDAL pipeline contract snapshot from the installed GDAL — gdalviz_refresh_contract
text: Runs gdal <type> pipeline --json-usage GDAL CLI user's PATH (explicit binary) writes result new contract snapshot, contract registry matches locally installed GDAL version instead snapshot bundled package.

title: Regenerate the GDAL pipeline contract snapshot from the installed GDAL — gdalviz_refresh_contract
code: gdalviz_refresh_contract(   path = NULL,   type = c("vector", "raster"),   gdal = NULL )

title: Regenerate the GDAL pipeline contract snapshot from the installed GDAL — gdalviz_refresh_contract
text: path Destination snapshot JSON. Defaults bundled snapshot location, replacing current installation. type Pipeline type ("vector" "raster"). gdal Path gdal binary. Defaults gdal PATH.

title: Regenerate the GDAL pipeline contract snapshot from the installed GDAL — gdalviz_refresh_contract
text: refreshed gdalviz_contract, invisibly.

what: Lint a GDAL pipeline against the GDAL contract — lint_pipeline
title: Lint a GDAL pipeline against the GDAL contract — lint_pipeline
text: Produces structured issue table unknown steps, unknown arguments, missing required arguments, value/type mismatches.

title: Lint a GDAL pipeline against the GDAL contract — lint_pipeline
Lint a GDAL pipeline against the GDAL contract — lint_pipeline
text: x gdalviz_pipeline, string/path accepted read_gdalg(). contract gdalviz_contract.

title: Lint a GDAL pipeline against the GDAL contract — lint_pipeline
text: tibble one row per lint issue.

what: Parse a GDAL vector pipeline into a structured object — parse_pipeline
title: Parse a GDAL vector pipeline into a structured object — parse_pipeline
text: Accepts raw pipeline command line (without gdal [vector|raster] pipeline prefix) parses ordered list steps, resolving nested pipelines ([ ... ]) associating arguments values using GDAL contract disambiguate boolean flags.

title: Parse a GDAL vector pipeline into a structured object — parse_pipeline
Parse a GDAL vector pipeline into a structured object — parse_pipeline
text: x pipeline command-line string. contract gdalviz_contract used disambiguate argument types.

title: Parse a GDAL vector pipeline into a structured object — parse_pipeline
text: gdalviz_pipeline object.

what: Parse a shell or powershell script into a pipeline — parse_script
title: Parse a shell or powershell script into a pipeline — parse_script
text: Normalizes gdal ... pipeline invocation written shell powershell script – joining line continuations, stripping comments, collapsing -strings / heredocs single tokens – parses parse_pipeline().

title: Parse a shell or powershell script into a pipeline — parse_script
code: parse_script(   text,   shell = c("bash", "powershell"),   contract = gdalviz_contract() )

title: Parse a shell or powershell script into a pipeline — parse_script
text: text script text (single string character vector lines). shell Source shell: "bash" (default) "powershell". contract gdalviz_contract.

title: Parse a shell or powershell script into a pipeline — parse_script
text: gdalviz_pipeline.

what: Shiny bindings for pipeline_flow widgets — pipelineFlowOutput
title: Shiny bindings for pipeline_flow widgets — pipelineFlowOutput
text: Output render functions using render_reactflow() widgets within Shiny applications.

title: Shiny bindings for pipeline_flow widgets — pipelineFlowOutput
code: pipelineFlowOutput(outputId, width = "100%", height = "560px")  renderPipelineFlow(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for pipeline_flow widgets — pipelineFlowOutput
text: outputId Output variable read . width, height CSS dimensions widget container. expr expression returns pipeline_flow widget. env environment evaluate expr. quoted Whether expr quoted expression.

title: Shiny bindings for pipeline_flow widgets — pipelineFlowOutput
text: pipelineFlowOutput() returns Shiny output element; renderPipelineFlow() returns Shiny render function.

what: Generate the DOT specification for a pipeline graph — pipeline_dot
title: Generate the DOT specification for a pipeline graph — pipeline_dot
text: Generate DOT specification pipeline graph

title: Generate the DOT specification for a pipeline graph — pipeline_dot
Generate the DOT specification for a pipeline graph — pipeline_dot
text: graph gdalviz_graph pipeline_graph(). direction Layout direction: "TB" (vertical, default) "LR". theme "light" "dark".

title: Generate the DOT specification for a pipeline graph — pipeline_dot
text: length-one character string DOT.

what: Build a renderer-agnostic graph model from a parsed pipeline — pipeline_graph
title: Build a renderer-agnostic graph model from a parsed pipeline — pipeline_graph
text: Converts parse_pipeline() result graph nodes edges, classifying step, rendering arguments code, generating plain-language description, propagating feature-stream state (CRS, geometry type, field schema, validity, ordering) along pipeline.

title: Build a renderer-agnostic graph model from a parsed pipeline — pipeline_graph
Build a renderer-agnostic graph model from a parsed pipeline — pipeline_graph
text: x gdalviz_pipeline (parse_pipeline()) string/path accepted read_gdalg(). contract gdalviz_contract. merge_repeated Merge runs 3+ consecutive identical commands (e.g. one-field---time set-field-type chains needed schema overrides) single stacked node. Defaults TRUE.

title: Build a renderer-agnostic graph model from a parsed pipeline — pipeline_graph
text: gdalviz_graph: list nodes edges tibbles.

what: Read a GDAL pipeline from a GDALG file or raw string — read_gdalg
title: Read a GDAL pipeline from a GDALG file or raw string — read_gdalg
text: Accepts path GDALG JSON file ({"type":"gdal_streamed_alg", "command_line": "..."}), path raw pipeline text file, raw pipeline string, returns parsed gdalviz_pipeline.

title: Read a GDAL pipeline from a GDALG file or raw string — read_gdalg
Read a GDAL pipeline from a GDALG file or raw string — read_gdalg
text: x path GDALG/JSON/text file, raw pipeline string. contract gdalviz_contract.

title: Read a GDAL pipeline from a GDALG file or raw string — read_gdalg
text: gdalviz_pipeline.

title: Read a shell or powershell script file into a pipeline — read_script
text: Read shell powershell script file pipeline

title: Read a shell or powershell script file into a pipeline — read_script
Read a shell or powershell script file into a pipeline — read_script
text: path Path .sh / .ps1 script. shell Source shell: "bash" (default) "powershell". contract gdalviz_contract.

title: Read a shell or powershell script file into a pipeline — read_script
text: gdalviz_pipeline.

what: Render a pipeline as a canonical GDALG command line — render_command_line
title: Render a pipeline as a canonical GDALG command line — render_command_line
text: Serializes parsed parse_pipeline() result back single-line command_line string, using minimal quoting GDAL uses writes *.gdalg.json files. result round-trips: parsing output yields equivalent pipeline.

title: Render a pipeline as a canonical GDALG command line — render_command_line
Render a pipeline as a canonical GDALG command line — render_command_line
text: x gdalviz_pipeline, string/path accepted read_gdalg(). prog Logical NULL. Whether emit leading gdal <type> pipeline program prefix. Defaults TRUE. type Pipeline type ("vector" "raster"). Defaults type detected parsing, falling back "vector".

title: Render a pipeline as a canonical GDALG command line — render_command_line
text: length-one character vector.

what: Render a pipeline graph as a Graphviz diagram (static-capable) — render_diagrammer
title: Render a pipeline graph as a Graphviz diagram (static-capable) — render_diagrammer
text: Builds DOT specification card-style HTML nodes (verb header, code body, plain-language description), category colors, state-annotated edges, rendered via DiagrammeR. Suitable static export SVG/PNG.

title: Render a pipeline graph as a Graphviz diagram (static-capable) — render_diagrammer
Render a pipeline graph as a Graphviz diagram (static-capable) — render_diagrammer
text: graph gdalviz_graph pipeline_graph(). direction Layout direction: "TB" (vertical, default) "LR". theme "light" "dark".

title: Render a pipeline graph as a Graphviz diagram (static-capable) — render_diagrammer
text: DiagrammeR grViz htmlwidget.

what: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive g6 (AntV G6) widget — render_g6
title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive g6 (AntV G6) widget — render_g6
text: Produces modern, interactive node-link diagram using dagre (hierarchical) layout. Nodes card-style, colored category, labelled step verb plain-language description; edges carry state-change badges.

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive g6 (AntV G6) widget — render_g6
code: render_g6(   graph,   direction = c("TB", "LR", "BT", "RL"),   theme = c("light", "dark"),   height = NULL,   width = "100%" )

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive g6 (AntV G6) widget — render_g6
text: graph gdalviz_graph pipeline_graph(). direction Layout direction: "TB" (default), "LR", "BT", "RL". theme "light" "dark". height, width Widget dimensions.

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive g6 (AntV G6) widget — render_g6
text: g6 htmlwidget.

what: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
text: Produces modern, interactive dataflow diagram GDAL pipeline using React Flow (xyflow). Steps render card-style nodes colored category arguments inline; edges carry state-change badges (CRS, geometry type, field count); tee branches render dashed side flows. Clicking node opens inspector panel full argument list, propagated stream state, link GDAL documentation step.

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
code: render_reactflow(   graph,   direction = c("TB", "LR", "BT", "RL"),   theme = c("light", "dark"),   minimap = TRUE,   controls = TRUE,   legend = TRUE,   draggable = TRUE,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   elementId = NULL )

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
text: graph gdalviz_graph pipeline_graph(), pipeline/string/path accepted . direction Layout direction: "TB" (default), "LR", "BT", "RL". theme "light" (default) "dark". minimap Show minimap overview. Defaults TRUE. controls Show zoom/fit controls. Defaults TRUE. legend Show category legend. Defaults TRUE. draggable Allow nodes repositioned dragging. Defaults TRUE. width, height Widget dimensions passed htmlwidgets::createWidget(). elementId Optional explicit element id widget container.

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
text: pipeline_flow htmlwidget.

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
text: JavaScript bundle built srcjs/ (see srcjs/README.md) shipped package, node toolchain needed run time.

title: Render a pipeline graph as an interactive React Flow widget — render_reactflow
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gdalg <- system.file(   "extdata", "pipelines", "tiger_states_gdalg.json",   package = "gdalviz" ) pipeline_graph(gdalg) |> render_reactflow(theme = "dark") } # }

what: Render a pipeline as a formatted shell script — render_script
title: Render a pipeline as a formatted shell script — render_script
text: Produces indented, line-continued gdal <type> pipeline invocation either bash/sh powershell, one ! step per line shell-appropriate quoting. Optionally reflows large SQL heredoc (bash) -string (powershell) readability.

title: Render a pipeline as a formatted shell script — render_script
code: render_script(   x,   shell = c("bash", "powershell"),   indent = "  ",   type = NULL,   globals_per_line = 3L,   sql = c("inline", "block") )

title: Render a pipeline as a formatted shell script — render_script
text: x gdalviz_pipeline, string/path accepted read_gdalg(). shell Target shell: "bash" (default) "powershell". indent Indentation unit steps. Defaults two spaces. type Pipeline type ("vector" "raster"). globals_per_line Number global options place per continuation line. Defaults 3. sql Either "inline" (default, single quoted token) "block" (multiline heredoc / -string --sql values).

title: Render a pipeline as a formatted shell script — render_script
text: length-one character vector containing full script.

what: Validate a GDAL pipeline against the GDAL contract — validate_pipeline
title: Validate a GDAL pipeline against the GDAL contract — validate_pipeline
text: Validates parsed pipeline returns structured validation object. default errors validation fails.

title: Validate a GDAL pipeline against the GDAL contract — validate_pipeline
Validate a GDAL pipeline against the GDAL contract — validate_pipeline
text: x gdalviz_pipeline, string/path accepted read_gdalg(). contract gdalviz_contract. strict TRUE (default), abort validation contains errors.

title: Validate a GDAL pipeline against the GDAL contract — validate_pipeline
text: gdalviz_validation object valid issues.

title: Write a pipeline to a GDALG JSON file — write_gdalg
text: Write pipeline GDALG JSON file

code: write_gdalg(x, path, relative_paths = TRUE, gdal_version = NULL, pretty = TRUE)

title: Write a pipeline to a GDALG JSON file — write_gdalg
text: x gdalviz_pipeline, string/path accepted read_gdalg(). path Output path (conventionally ending .gdalg.json). relative_paths Logical relative_paths_relative_to_this_file member. Defaults TRUE. gdal_version Optional GDAL version string record. pretty Whether pretty-print JSON. Defaults TRUE.

title: Write a pipeline to a GDALG JSON file — write_gdalg
text: path, invisibly.
